explanation: The property states that when the system is in "PSV Mode", and the "inspiratory phase" is active, and either the "inspiration peak is reached" (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or the "max_insp_time_psv is over" (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime), then the "expiration phase begins" in the very next step. This captures the transition from the inspiratory phase to the expiratory phase based on the specified termination conditions. The "lasts until" part is implicitly captured by requiring the inspiratoryPhase to be active when the termination condition is met for the transition to occur.

The property captures that, within 'PSV Mode', whenever the 'inspiration peak is reached' or the 'max_insp_time_psv is over', the 'expiration phase' must begin in the very next state. This directly translates the 'After that the expiration phase begins' clause, with the specified conditions acting as the trigger. While the 'lasts until' part implicitly describes the duration of the inspiratory phase leading up to these triggers, the chosen template focuses on the immediate consequence of these conditions, ensuring the correct transition to the expiratory phase.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV mode AND the inspiration phase's termination condition is met (either the inspiration peak is reached, or the maximum inspiration time is over), then in the very next state, the expiration phase must begin. This captures the transition from inspiration to expiration based on the specified criteria in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode and either the inspiration peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or the maximum inspiratory time is over (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime), then in the very next state (X), the expiration phase must begin (expiratoryPhaseStart). This captures the specified condition-response behavior for the transition from inspiration to expiration, ensuring the expiration phase starts immediately after the inspiration phase termination conditions are met in PSV Mode.

The LTL property captures that in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, whenever the inspiratory peak is reached or the maximum inspiration time is exceeded, the expiration phase must begin in the very next state. The phrase "lasts until" implies that the mentioned conditions (inspiration peak or max inspiration time) are the valid termination events for the inspiration phase, leading to the next phase.

The LTL property states that, globally, if the inspiration peak is reached OR the maximum inspiration time (max_insp_time_psv) is over (which are the conditions for the inspiration phase to end), then the expiration phase must begin in the very next state. This directly translates 'the inspiration phase lasts until the inspiration peak is reached but no later than the max_insp_time_psv is over. After that the expiration phase begins.' by defining the combined termination condition as the antecedent and the start of the expiration phase as the immediate consequent.

The property states that when the system is in PSV mode and the inspiratory phase is active, this phase must continue until either the inspiration peak is reached or the maximum inspiration time for PSV mode is over. The chosen LTL template (ID 99) directly uses the Until operator to capture this "lasts until" aspect of the inspiratory phase duration and its termination conditions. It ensures that if the system is in PSV mode and the inspiratory phase is active (modeled together as bool_exp1), this combined state will persist until the specified end condition (bool_exp3) is met. The 'After that the expiration phase begins' part implies a direct temporal consequence. While the chosen template accurately models the preceding 'lasts until' condition, its structure does not allow for explicit immediate (Next) temporal consequences of the 'until' condition within the same property due to constraints on boolean expressions not containing LTL operators. Thus, this property primarily focuses on the duration and termination of the inspiratory phase within PSV mode.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) | (X ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))) & (bool_exp1 -> ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))

explanation:
The property states that "Globally, if the system is in PSV Mode and the inspiration peak is reached or the max inspiration time is over, then in the next state, the expiration phase begins." 
- "PSV Mode": This is captured by bool_exp1 being PSVMode, ensuring the property applies only in this mode.
- "the inspiration peak is reached but no later than the max_insp_time_psv is over": This defines the conditions for the end of the inspiration phase. It is captured by bool_exp2 as P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime. The "but no later than" implies that either condition can terminate the phase.
- "After that the expiration phase begins": This indicates an immediate transition to the expiration phase once the termination condition is met. This is captured by bool_exp3 being expiratoryPhaseStart and the X (next) operator in the LTL template.
While the phrase "The inspiration phase lasts until..." implies that inspiratoryPhase must hold continuously until the termination condition, the chosen LTL primarily focuses on the precise trigger and immediate consequence described by "After that...". In the context of a sequential system, the initiation of the next phase (expiration) upon the specified conditions implicitly signifies the end of the preceding phase (inspiration).

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV Mode AND either the inspiration peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or the maximum inspiratory time (max_insp_time_psv, represented by inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) is over, then the expiration phase (expiratoryPhaseStart) must begin in the very next state. This accurately captures the condition for the inspiration phase ending and immediately transitioning to the expiration phase.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'PSV Mode' AND (either the 'inspiration peak is reached' (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) OR the 'max_insp_time_psv is over' (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime)), then in the very next state, the 'expiration phase must begin' (expiratoryPhaseStart). This captures the conditions that terminate the inspiration phase and immediately trigger the start of the expiration phase within the PSV mode.
